mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative in periods when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, with a t-test p > 0.05 against baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) reflects rising demand for residential construction, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when HOUST growth exceeds 5% over 2 months, with a t-test p > 0.05 against baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month rise in unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when UNRATE exceeds 4%, with a t-test p > 0.05 against baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals strong demand pressures that drive equity market optimism, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative when both GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL show positive growth, with a t-test p > 0.05 against baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate above 5% indicates monetary tightening, which raises equity discount rates and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, with a t-test p > 0.05 against baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding supply-side capacity, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 1% over 2 months, with a t-test p > 0.05 against baseline returns.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer credit growth (RSAFS) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong consumer demand and labor market tightness, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% and UNRATE is below 3.5%, with a t-test p > 0.05 against baseline returns.
